name: Week 13 KB Full Body — Heal Week, Full Variety (Spotlight: C&P + TGU) + NEW Brutal Complex
description: W13 is a **shoulder-heal week** — the heavy PPL volume backs off and the right-shoulder cluster gets a beat. The KB day is **NOT trimmed**: KB flow/skill work isn't what's fatiguing (the shoulder + heavy lifting are), so this day keeps full variety. The @Asgooch overhead mobility complex runs its full **5 rounds** per side, and a **NEW non-overhead conditioning block — the @Asgooch Brutal Complex** (Swing → AB-stance goblet squat → alternating row → close-grip pushup) — joins as a finisher for fresh variety, deliberately non-overhead so it adds work without taxing the healing shoulder. Spotlights cycle back to **C&P + TGU** per the rotation tracker — a refresh of technique cues. Cossack + windmill drop to maintenance after their W12 spotlight. **W12 takeaways:** the Asgooch flow landed well; C&P hit 45 cleanly ('everything went great'); TGU both sides 'feel fine now' (W12D4).

dayOverview.theme: Shoulder-heal week — but the KB day keeps FULL variety, not trimmed (flow work isn't the fatigue source). Asgooch flow runs all 5 rounds; a new non-overhead Brutal Complex finisher adds fresh conditioning. Spotlights cycle back to C&P + TGU — technique refresh. Cossack + windmill go to maintenance. C&P holds 45, TGU holds 30. The only heal-week concession is keeping overhead-SHOULDER volume managed — modify the Asgooch overhead moves if the shoulder talks.

spotlights.why: Cycles back to spotlight per the rotation tracker. W11/W12 confirmed 45 grooves cleanly ('everything went great'). Deload week refreshes the technique cues at 45 — knee-bent catch, hips drive the clean, ribs down on the press — without the pull toward 50. The cue is the work.

Cycles back to spotlight. W12D4: 'TGU on both sides feel fine now' — the W12 left-side checkpoint drilling did its job, the left is no longer the weak link. W13 drills full checkpoint discipline at 30lb on both sides equally — the straight-arm overhead lockout, pause at every checkpoint, slow reverse. Refresh, not rehab.

dayOverview.rotationPlan: W11 swing mechanics + bent press leverage · W12 windmill + cossack mobility · W13 C&P + TGU (current, DELOAD refresh) · W14 cycle forward — bent press / flow work returns.
watchpoints: **W13 is a shoulder-heal week — but the KB day is NOT shortened.** Full variety stays: Asgooch flow 5 rounds, plus the new Brutal Complex finisher. The heal applies to LOADS — C&P holds 45 (no 50), TGU holds 30, no load chasing. Variety is rich; weight is held.

**TGU is a STRAIGHT-ARM lift.** Punch the bell to a stable straight-arm overhead lockout — the arm stays locked vertical the whole way up and down. There is NO rack hold during the get-up (the clean-to-rack is only the brief setup before pressing overhead). Wobble at the lockout = wobble through the lift.

**Right shoulder watch through all overhead work** — the Asgooch flow stacks 4 overhead moves. If the shoulder talks during the snatch or OH lunge, drop the weight or modify (front-rack lunge instead of OH lunge).
**Overhead squat is the hardest move in the flow** — if mobility limits, REDUCE DEPTH before reducing load.
Cossack + windmill are maintenance this week — sharp cues only, both at 25lb, range is the work.

exercises.notes: **LOAD:** 15-20lb. Big slow circles — open the shoulders before loading overhead.
**LOAD:** 20lb. Light — test the overhead position. If anything cracks/twinges, do extra band warmup before the flow.
**LOAD:** 25lb. Open the hips. Sit in the bottom for a beat.

sets.name: Banded Shoulder Prep (~3 min)

exercises.notes: Mini-band OR blue rope band held in hands — both work.
Elbows drive to the ribs, scaps depress.
Don't overarch the lower back to chase range.
15s each side. Primes the lateral hinge.
Both arms in a V/Y, hold and breathe. Lower traps fire — preps the overhead lockout for the flow and the TGU.

sets.notes: 1 round of all 7 banded moves (~3 min). Sustained tension, no rest between positions.

sets.name: Block 1: Asgooch Overhead Mobility Complex — LEFT SIDE (5 Rounds — Full Variety Kept)

exercises.notes: **LOAD:** 20-25lb (start at 25 if smooth, drop to 20 if the right shoulder warmup felt off). Left hand. Controlled pull, full extension, hips hinged, back flat.
Left hand. **Hips drive the bell**, not the arm. Straight overhead, punch through at the top — smooth catch sets up the next 3 moves.
Left hand overhead. **Lock the elbow, bell over the shoulder, torso TALL.** Drive through the front HEEL. **Right shoulder talks → modify to a front-rack lunge** on this move only.
Left hand overhead. **Hardest move in the flow.** Full depth IF mobility allows; **reduce depth before load** if mobility limits. Bell stacked over the shoulder, core braced, no lower-back arching.
Left hand. **Dip and drive** — legs do the work, arm guides. Smooth descent to the rack. End of one round.

sets.notes: **5 rounds LEFT side**, all moves continuous, no rest within a round. Bell stays in the working hand for the whole round. Rest 30-45s between rounds. **Weak side first** — left gets the freshest reps.

sets.name: Block 1 (continued): Asgooch Overhead Mobility Complex — RIGHT SIDE (5 Rounds)

exercises.notes: Right hand. Controlled pull, full extension.
Right hand. Hips drive, punch through at the top.
Right hand overhead. **Right shoulder watchpoint** — most likely place for the crackle. Front-rack lunge if needed.
Right hand overhead. Same depth/load discipline as the left side.
Right hand. Finish strong. End of the complex.

sets.notes: **5 rounds RIGHT side** after the left is done. Same rest pattern. **Right shoulder watchpoint** — the snatch + OH lunge + OH squat stack the overhead position; if the shoulder talks, modify (front-rack lunge, front squat) rather than grinding. This is the one heal-week concession — keep the overhead moves clean, don't push through a cranky shoulder.

sets.name: Block 2: Cossack + Windmill (Maintenance — sharp cues only)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 25lb goblet (HOLD). Maintenance — spotlight was W12.
- Sit DEEP, heels stay down
- Working leg in full external rotation
- Slow descent, hard stop at end-range — that's the work, not failure
- Range, not load

**LOAD:** 25lb (HOLD). Maintenance — spotlight was W12.
- Bell locked overhead, eye tracks the bell
- **Hip hinge AWAY from the bell side**
- **Stretch lives in the back leg's hip flexor, NOT the lower back**
- Long spine throughout

**LOAD:** 30 (HOLD — light; the Asgooch flow already loaded the shoulder, and it's a deload). 2 rounds.

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — refresh the checkpoint discipline (W12D4: both sides feel fine now, so drill BOTH equally):**
- **The TGU is a STRAIGHT-ARM lift** — punch the bell to a stable straight-arm overhead lockout and keep the arm locked vertical the whole way up and down. There is NO rack hold during the get-up. If the overhead lockout is wobbly, the rest of the lift inherits the wobble.
- **Eyes ON the bell** at every checkpoint — eyes leaving the bell = bell drift
- **Pause at every checkpoint** — lying → elbow → hand → hip bridge → leg sweep → lunge → stand → reverse. No rushing.
- **Hip bridge: glute squeeze, ribs down** (no flare — rib flare = lower-back load)
- **Reverse with the same discipline as forward** — the descent is half the lift
- **'Focus on the clean itself'** (the W10D2 unlock) — the setup IS the work

**Pattern:** 2 forward + 2 reverse per side, left side first. **Reflection:** with both sides clean now, does the checkpoint discipline hold up under no-rush attention, or does a checkpoint still get skipped when the focus drifts? The deload is the week to make every checkpoint deliberate.

sets.name: 🔆 Block 4: Clean & Press (Spotlight — Technique Refresh at 45)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD:** 45 (HOLD — W11/W12 confirmed 45 grooves cleanly, 'everything went great'). **NO 50 — it's a deload, and 50 was never the W13 plan.** 2 rounds.

**🔆 SPOTLIGHT — refresh the technique cues at 45:**
- **Catch the clean with the knees slightly bent** — the W3 cue, durable: the knee bend absorbs the clean so there's zero impact at the rack
- **Hips drive the clean**, NOT the arm — the arm just guides the bell into the rack
- **Press: ribs DOWN** (the W8 trigger) — no rib flare under the press
- **Bicep finishes by the ear, full lockout** at the top
- **Lower with control — no slam** back to the rack

**Symmetry check:** if the left at 45 feels rougher than the right, that's normal (right is ahead) — but if it's a big gap, do the left reps slower. Quality wins. **Reflection:** at a held 45 with full attention on the cues, does the clean feel automatic? If yes, that's the technique grooved — W14+ can revisit 50.

sets.name: 🆕 Block 5: Figure-8 → Tactical Clean (Skill Flow — Now Practiced Twice a Week)

exercises.notes:
**LOAD: 12kg (26lb).** 2 rounds of 60s continuous flow.

The figure-8-tactical-clean is ONE compound move — the figure-8 pass between the legs exits into a clean to rack, alternating sides:
- START in the hike position (bell between the legs, hinged at the hips)
- **Figure-8 → exit into a tactical clean to rack** — pass between the legs, hand catches BEHIND, the pull becomes the clean
- Lower the bell back through hike; the next pass switches sides
- Continue alternating for the full 60s

**Cues:**
- **The figure-8 EXITS into the clean** — the bell doesn't return to a hand mid-pass; the catch becomes the clean
- **Tactical clean = controlled, threaded into the rack** — no yank, no forearm slam
- **Hike is home base** — every transition resets through hike
- **Right shoulder watch on the rack catch** — if it talks, drop to figure-8 passes only and finish the round

This is the W12D4 flow you liked — added to Tuesday so you're drilling it twice a week (here + Saturday's kb-cond). Skill practice, light bell, keep it smooth.

sets.notes: Light figure-8 skill block — 2 rounds at 12kg, ~3 min work + rest. You're now practicing this flow twice weekly (Tuesday + Saturday). The pass exits INTO the clean — that's the skill being drilled.

sets.name: 🆕 Block 6: Brutal Complex Finisher (@Asgooch — NEW, Non-Overhead Conditioning)

exercises.notes: Hips snap the bell to chest height — it's a hinge, not a squat, not a front raise. Shoulders packed down.
Bell at the chest, offset (B-stance) goblet squat — most of the load on the front leg, the back foot is a kickstand. Sit tall, full depth. Switch the front leg each round.
Hinged over, flat back, row one side then the other. Pull the elbow to the hip, no torso rotation.
Hands close (on the floor or on the bell handle). Ribs down, body in one line, slow. Scale to the knees if the rounds get ragged.

sets.notes: **🆕 NEW — the @Asgooch Brutal Complex, added W13 for fresh conditioning variety.** Swing → AB-stance goblet squat → alternating row → close-grip pushup, 10 reps each, **continuous — don't put the bell down between moves.** 3 rounds. **Deliberately non-overhead** — it adds a real conditioning hit without loading the healing shoulder overhead. Single bell, moderate weight (start ~25-30lb). This is the 'metabolism going' kind of block — pace it, but keep moving.

exercises.notes: **LOAD:** 25lb. Light row, 2s pull / 2s lower. Skip if the shoulders are cooked from the flow.
60s each side. Hip flexor release.
60s. Adductors get tight on KB days from cossacks.

tips: **W13 is a shoulder-heal week — but the KB day keeps full variety, not trimmed.** Asgooch flow runs all 5 rounds; the heal applies to LOADS (C&P 45, TGU 30, no chasing), not to variety.

**🆕 NEW: Brutal Complex finisher** (@Asgooch — swing → AB-stance goblet squat → alt row → close-grip pushup, 3 rounds). Non-overhead by design — fresh conditioning that doesn't tax the healing shoulder.
**Figure-8 → tactical clean flow added to Tuesday** — you're now drilling the W12D4 flow you liked twice a week (Tue here + Sat kb-cond). Light skill practice, 12kg.
**Spotlights cycle back to C&P + TGU** — a technique refresh of two lifts that already grooved well. Drill the cues at held loads.
**TGU is a STRAIGHT-ARM lift** — straight-arm overhead lockout, no rack hold during the get-up. Both sides feel fine now (W12D4) — drill checkpoint discipline equally.
**C&P: knee-bent catch, hips drive the clean, ribs down on the press.** 45 is the held working weight — 50 is a post-deload conversation.
**Cossack + windmill are maintenance** — sharp cues only, both at 25lb, range is the work.
**Right shoulder watch through all overhead work** — modify the OH lunge to a front-rack lunge if the shoulder talks.
Rotation plan: W14 cycles forward — bent press / flow work returns.
formPhilosophy: Shoulder-heal week — but the KB day is where variety lives, not where volume gets cut. Full Asgooch flow + a new non-overhead conditioning complex. The heal is in the held loads and the managed overhead-shoulder volume, not in a shortened session. The C&P + TGU spotlight refreshes cues that already work.
